cards.front: Which Article is called the 'Heart and Soul of the Constitution'?
cards.back: Article 32 — Right to Constitutional Remedies. Called so by Dr. B.R. Ambedkar.

cards.front: Article 21A was added by which Amendment and guarantees what?
cards.back: 86th Amendment (2002) — Free and compulsory education for children aged 6–14 years.

cards.front: When was the Indian Constitution adopted? When did it come into force?
cards.back: Adopted: 26 November 1949. Came into force: 26 January 1950 (Republic Day).

cards.front: Which Amendment added 'Socialist', 'Secular', and 'Integrity' to the Preamble?
cards.back: 42nd Constitutional Amendment, 1976.

cards.front: What does Article 356 deal with?
cards.back: President's Rule — imposition of central rule in a state when constitutional machinery fails.

cards.front: Who is the highest law officer of India?
cards.back: Attorney General of India (Article 76). Not to be confused with Advocate General (Art 165) who is the highest law officer of a state.

cards.front: The Right to Property was removed from Fundamental Rights by which Amendment?
cards.back: 44th Amendment (1978). It is now a Constitutional Right under Article 300A.

cards.front: How many members can the Rajya Sabha have at maximum?
cards.back: 250 members — 238 elected by state/UT legislatures + 12 nominated by President.

cards.front: Panchayati Raj was constitutionalized by which Amendment and which Article?
cards.back: 73rd Amendment (1992) — Article 243 onwards.

cards.front: Which body replaced the Planning Commission in 2015?
cards.back: NITI Aayog (National Institution for Transforming India). Planning Commission was abolished.

cards.front: Article 17 deals with?
cards.back: Abolition of Untouchability — forbids its practice in any form.

cards.front: Who presides over a Joint Sitting of Parliament?
cards.back: Speaker of Lok Sabha.

cards.front: What is the voting age in India and which amendment changed it?
cards.back: 18 years. Changed from 21 to 18 by the 61st Amendment (1988).

cards.front: Finance Commission is established under which Article? How often?
cards.back: Article 280. Constituted every 5 years to recommend tax devolution between Centre and States.

cards.front: What does the 52nd Amendment (1985) deal with?
cards.back: Anti-defection law — 10th Schedule of the Constitution.

cards.front: Can Rajya Sabha be dissolved?
cards.back: NO. Rajya Sabha is a permanent House. Only Lok Sabha can be dissolved by the President.

cards.front: Article 44 deals with?
cards.back: Uniform Civil Code — it is a Directive Principle (non-justiciable, state must endeavour).

cards.front: Who appoints the Governor of a state?
cards.back: The President of India (on the advice of Central Government). NOT the state legislature.

cards.front: Supreme Court is established under which Article?
cards.back: Article 124.

cards.front: What is the Election Commission of India established under?
cards.back: Article 324. It is a Constitutional body — independent, not under government control.

cards.front: Parts III and IV of the Constitution deal with?
cards.back: Part III: Fundamental Rights (Art 12–35). Part IV: Directive Principles (Art 36–51).

cards.front: Which amendment introduced GST?
cards.back: 101st Constitutional Amendment, 2016.

cards.front: What is the 9th Schedule and which amendment created it?
cards.back: 1st Amendment (1951). 9th Schedule contains laws protected from judicial review (land reform laws).

cards.front: Article 368 deals with?
cards.back: Amendment of the Constitution. Parliament can amend by special majority.

cards.front: What is the term of a Rajya Sabha member?
cards.back: 6 years. One-third of members retire every 2 years. The House itself never dissolves.

cards.front: Under which Article can the President declare a National Emergency?
cards.back: Article 352 — on grounds of War, External Aggression, or Armed Rebellion.

cards.front: Fundamental Duties are listed in which Article?
cards.back: Article 51A — added by the 42nd Amendment 1976. There are 11 Fundamental Duties.

cards.front: What does Article 123 deal with?
cards.back: Ordinance-making power of the President — when Parliament is not in session.

cards.front: The CAG (Comptroller and Auditor General) is established under which Article?
cards.back: Article 148. CAG audits all accounts of the Union and States.

cards.front: 10% EWS Reservation was added by which Amendment?
cards.back: 103rd Constitutional Amendment, 2019.
